mnemonics: BoS: Battle of the Spouses. Remember it's a coordination game with two pure Nash equilibria where players want to coordinate but have different preferred outcomes, like a couple deciding on an evening out (Bach concert vs. Stravinsky ballet).
keyPoints: The Bach or Stravinsky (BoS) game is a two-player coordination game.
It has two pure-strategy Nash equilibria: (Bach, Bach) and (Stravinsky, Stravinsky).
Players have a common interest in coordinating but a conflict over which equilibrium is better (Player 1 prefers Bach, Player 2 prefers Stravinsky).
commonMistakes: Misidentifying it as a zero-sum or pure conflict game like Prisoner's Dilemma; it is a coordination game with a conflict of interest.
Forgetting that (Bach, Stravinsky) and (Stravinsky, Bach) are not equilibria because both players would want to deviate from those outcomes.
